title: Celestial Navigation via Rational Trigonometry — 7 star sights + two-star fix
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that celestial navigation sight reduction can be expressed entirely in spreads, crosses, and discrete orientation flags. Seven star witnesses verified: RT s_altitude matches classical sin²(h) to float precision. Two-star fix demonstrates algebraic position determination without trig inversion. Sextant = spread measurement instrument.
_derivation: Classical: sin(h) = sin(φ)sin(δ) + cos(φ)cos(δ)cos(LHA). RT: s_h = [σ₁√(s_φ·s_δ) + σ₂√(c_φ·c_δ·c_LHA)]² where σ₁,σ₂ ∈ {±1} are discrete orientation flags. σ₁ = sign(sin(φ)sin(δ)) = same-hemisphere flag. σ₂ = sign(cos(LHA)) = quadrant flag. The decomposition into unsigned spreads + discrete signs IS Theorem NT: continuous → (quadratic measure + discrete choice).

observer.label: London (51.5°N, 0°W)
observer._note: All sights taken from London for consistency with [156] WGS84 and [164] gnomonic witnesses.

witnesses.label: Polaris (α UMi)
witnesses._note: Pole star nearly overhead. h ≈ φ (altitude ≈ latitude). s_Az = 0 (due north).

witnesses.label: Sirius (α CMa)
witnesses._note: Southern star. σ₁=-1 (opposite hemispheres). Low altitude h=11.3°.

witnesses.label: Canopus (α Car)
witnesses._note: Far southern, on anti-meridian. BELOW HORIZON (h=-88.8°). s_alt near 1.0 = star at nadir.

witnesses.label: Sun (equinox, noon)
witnesses._note: Sun at equinox on meridian. h = 90° - φ = 38.5°. Classical noon sight.

witnesses.label: Rigel (β Ori)
witnesses._note: Slightly south of equator. σ₁=-1. h=25.0°.

witnesses.label: Capella (α Aur)
witnesses._note: High northern, near meridian. h=81.4°. Near-zenith passage.

two_star_fix._note: Observer at unknown position measures altitudes of two stars. The intersection of two position circles (spread loci) gives the fix. Both equations are algebraic in spreads — no trig inversion needed.

stars.name: Vega
stars._note: Position circle: all points where sin²(altitude_to_Vega) = 0.6946

stars.name: Arcturus
stars._note: Position circle: all points where sin²(altitude_to_Arcturus) = 0.1292

true_position._note: Both spread loci intersect at (51.5°N, 0°W) — the algebraic fix matches the true position.

qa_connection.sextant_is_spread_instrument: A sextant measures the double-reflected angle between horizon and star. The quantity sin²(altitude) = spread of altitude IS the fundamental measurement. The navigator is measuring spreads, not angles.
qa_connection.sigma_is_discrete_qa: The orientation flags σ₁ (hemisphere) and σ₂ (quadrant) are discrete 1-bit choices — exactly the kind of state QA handles. The decomposition sin(h) = continuous → s_h + σ₁ + σ₂ = (quadratic measure) + (discrete orientation) IS Theorem NT: the continuous function decomposes into an observer projection.
qa_connection.position_circle_is_spread_locus: A position circle = all points where spread(star, zenith) = s_observed. This is an algebraic curve in spread coordinates. Two such curves intersect algebraically — the fix requires no trig inversion, only solving spread equations.
qa_connection.wgs84_link: All sights computed on the WGS84 quantum ellipse [156]. The ellipsoidal corrections to celestial nav (dip, refraction) can also be expressed in spread form.
qa_connection.theorem_nt: Continuous angles → spreads (unsigned quadratic) + σ flags (discrete orientation). The boundary is crossed at measurement (sextant → spread) and at fix (spread equations → lat/lon). Between these: pure algebraic computation.

sources: Bowditch, American Practical Navigator (2019 ed.), Chapter 22: Sight Reduction
Nautical Almanac Office, HM Nautical Almanac (star positions)
Wildberger, Divine Proportions (2005), rational trig framework
